text.title: AWS Three-Tier Architecture (VPC, ALB, App, Database) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in AWS Well-Architected Reliability + Security pillars.

rule.statement: Prod VPC MUST span ≥2 AZs per tier (public / private / database). Single-AZ prod is forbidden.
rule.rationale: AZ failure takes down all resources in that AZ simultaneously. Multi-AZ is the minimum for any SLO above 99.9%.

rule.statement: Prod MUST deploy one NAT Gateway per AZ. Single shared NAT GW is forbidden in prod.
rule.rationale: A single NAT GW is an AZ-level SPOF that blocks all outbound traffic from private subnets if its AZ fails. ~$33/mo extra per AZ vs full outage.

rule.statement: Security groups MUST reference each other by SG ID (not by CIDR) for inter-tier traffic.
rule.rationale: SG-by-CIDR drifts when subnets change; SG-by-ID is self-documenting + survives CIDR refactors.

rule.statement: Database subnets MUST have no route to the internet (no IGW route, no NAT route). DB SG MUST only accept traffic from the app SG.
rule.rationale: Internet-routable DB subnets enable data exfiltration on credential compromise. Isolated subnets force exfil through the app layer (auditable).

rule.statement: New relational workloads MUST default to Aurora Serverless v2 unless ACU limits / extension support require provisioned Aurora.
rule.rationale: Aurora SLv2 removes DBA capacity-planning toil; scales 0.5 → 32+ ACU in seconds; ~50% cheaper for bursty workloads.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
